## Important: Breaking Change / 重要：破坏性变更

This release changes how VibeAround reads and normalizes local configuration. Some configuration fields have been merged, and some non-essential runtime/log data is no longer useful. For now, the recommended migration is a manual cleanup:

本版本修改了 VibeAround 本地配置的读取和归一化逻辑。部分配置项被合并，部分非必要的运行日志/缓存内容也不再有用。目前建议按下面流程手动清理：

`~/.vibearound/profiles`
`~/.vibearound/settings.json`
`~/.vibearound`
`profiles`
`settings.json`
`~/.vibearound`

results.title: GitHub - jazzenchen/VibeAround: Keep your AI coding agents around. Launch Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Pi Agent, and more from one place — side by side, connected, reachable, and ready to work across web, mobile, and messaging. · GitHub
results.content:
## Upgrade Notes

### v0.7.10 Breaking Change

VibeAround v0.7.10 changes how local configuration is read and normalized. Some configuration fields have been merged, and some non-essential runtime/log data is no longer useful. For now, the recommended migration is a manual cleanup:

`~/.vibearound/profiles`
`~/.vibearound/settings.json`
`~/.vibearound`
`profiles`
`settings.json`
`~/.vibearound`

Known impact: API Bridge configuration may be lost during this migration. Re-enable API Bridge on the affected profiles to restore it.

## Develop Locally

Prerequisites: Rust 1.82+, Bun 1.3+, and Node.js 24 LTS recommended. macOS also needs Xcode command line tools; Linux needs the WebKitGTK/Tauri system dependencies for your distribution.

## Documentation [...] VibeAround WeChat group QR code, valid until July 6, 2026

This WeChat QR code is valid until July 6, 2026. Use Discord or GitHub Issues to ask for the latest one if it has expired.

## About

Keep your AI coding agents around. Launch Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Pi Agent, and more from one place — side by side, connected, reachable, and ready to work across web, mobile, and messaging.

Documentation is still under construction and may lag behind fast-moving features.

## Community

VibeAround is still early, moving fast, and mostly one-person work. My goal is to build an all-in-one agent hub that helps more people embrace AI with less friction and more clarity. Some edges are still rough, from test coverage to interaction details, and I sincerely hope VibeAround can be shaped together with the community. Discussions, code contributions, ideas, shared workflows, and bug reports are always welcome.

README.md

## Frequently Asked Questions

### What is VibeAround?

VibeAround is an open-source ai agents skill for AI coding assistants such as Claude Code, Codex CLI, and ChatGPT, built by jazzenchen. Keep your AI coding agents around. Launch Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Pi Agent, and more from one place — side by side, connected, reachable, and ready to work across web, mobile, and messaging. It has 461 GitHub stars.

### Is VibeAround safe to use? [...] ### Is VibeAround safe to use?

Yes. VibeAround passed SkillsLLM's automated security scan — a dependency vulnerability audit plus prompt-injection heuristics — with no high-severity issues. You can read the full report in the Security Report section on this page.

### How do I install VibeAround?

Clone the repository with "git clone  and add it to your Claude Code skills directory (see the Installation section above).

### What programming language is VibeAround written in?

VibeAround is primarily written in Rust. It is open-source under jazzenchen on GitHub, so you can review or fork the full source.

### Are there alternatives to VibeAround? [...] ## Known Issue

### Why do I see `Unable to connect to API (ConnectionRefused)`?

This usually means the agent is still reading API settings written by CC Switch or another config-switching tool. Those settings can point the agent to a local proxy that is no longer running, so the agent refuses the connection before VibeAround can route the request.

Remove the conflicting fields from the corresponding agent config file, then launch the agent again from VibeAround. For Claude Code, check `~/.claude/settings.json` and remove stale provider fields such as an `env` block that sets a base URL, API key, or proxy endpoint. If you also have project-level Claude config files, check those as well.

## API Profiles & Bridge

results.title: Vibe coding can be really frustrating. Not because AI tools aren’t working. Because they often don’t do what you meant but just what you said. My IDE is filled with prompts like: “Try again” “Fix… | Roy Derks
results.content:
Like Reply  1 Reaction

Conor Corcoran 

Head of International Sales – AI Sisters | International Sales & Business Development | AI, Compliance & Agent Development | SaaS · Enterprise Technology | Paris & Europe

 1mo 

 Report this comment

I think the harsh reality is that the agents can't do the vibe end of things because is a computer and we don't speak the same language. It's a lot more complicated than is advertised. I have tried a lot of them and would love to hear people experiences with brittle builds and endless restarted the server loops. We don't know what the guard rails are and even when I paste code in it doesn't handle it correctly.

Like Reply  1 Reaction

Mekki MacAulay [...] 4mo 

 Report this comment

I like the sound of this tool, will definitely check it out. I’ve been reflecting more on this frustration and how to turn it into an opportunity. Lately I’ve been using a combination of markdown and bash guidelines to address misunderstandings. Cursor rules are cool (making cursor write its own rules, even cooler) Right now my general methodology is to write a spec with the LLM first, then go until I hit one of these “try again” moments. We both exchange Q&A to fix the misunderstanding, and then the spec is updated and we probably also write a test. A lot of time, I THROW AWAY the code at this point and start over, now with a more rigid spec and maybe a test case. ie, it’s more worthwhile to iterate on the instructions and then try again. [...] Vibe coding can be really frustrating. Not because AI tools aren’t working. Because they often don’t do what you meant but just what you said. My IDE is filled with prompts like: “Try again” “Fix this” “No, that’s not what I meant” Why don't they work? Most coding agents (Claude, Cursor, Lovable, etc.) have trouble seeing past the prompts you write, and get overwhelmed when there's too much information to digest. And lately I’ve found a better way to work with them: Give them clearer tasks. In smaller pieces. I came across this great open-source tool called claude-task-master that helps split your project into agent-friendly tasks. It’s already gained nearly 20k stars which shows I’m not the only one tired of “vibing” my way through code fixes. If you're using coding agents, give this a

All tunnel URLs are gated by a pairing-code auth flow — browsers must verify ownership via a connected IM channel before getting access. [...] VibeAround v0.6.3 is out.

This release wraps up the bigger v0.6.x shift: VibeAround is becoming a hub for coding agents across desktop, web, terminal, and messaging.

The biggest visible change since v0.5.x is the Web experience: it is now a web-based workspace for running different coding agents side by side, one place to launch, resume, inspect, and switch between agents.
